channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What Hollywood’s next potential merger means for streaming
item.description: One of Hollywood’s crown jewels is on the block: Warner Bros. Discovery, the parent company of HBO, CNN, and major movie franchises like Harry Potter and the DC Universe, officially confirmed this week that it is open to a sale.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Here are 20-plus kid tools for higher-quality screen time
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Not everything creative needs a prompt. The web is increasingly flooded with AI-generated images and videos, much of it aimed at kids.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: The new MacBook Pro: Still a workhorse
item.description: Hello again, and thank you, as always, for spending time with Fast Company’s Plugged In. Apple is legendary for figuring out what people want before they realize they want it. But since 2021, its MacBook Pro hasn’t been like that at all.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration pushes to get AI data centers on the grid faster
item.description: The Trump administration issued a proposal late Thursday that aims to help AI data centers get connected to the electric grid more quickly.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Intel sees supply shortage, will prioritize data center technology
item.description: Intel reported better than expected Q3 figures, reflecting a turnaround in sales along with cutting operational expenses and a renewed focus on its core business. On the downside, Intel is enjoying a little too much success and is seeing a shortage of supply.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft adds Copilot Mode to Edge as AI browser race heats up
item.description: Microsoft is enhancing its Edge browser with Copilot Mode to strengthen its AI capabilities, as it struggles to compete with Chrome’s dominance and a wave of emerging AI-native browsers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fortnite leaks point to a The Simpsons-themed mini-season starting in November, and I’m genuinely worried for my wallet
item.description: Leaks point toward a The Simpsons crossover in Fortnite. A new key art image has been leaked, showing Homer Simpson and other skins.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: The iPhone 18 Pro could come with a big Starlink upgrade – and it sounds perfect for off-grid adventures
item.description: We're starting to hear a few rumors around what Apple may have planned for the next set of flagship iPhones.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI is connecting all company secrets to ChatGPT - all your work data and apps are now open
item.description: Company knowledge, a self-explanatory ChatGPT tool, will cite internal documents for accurate reporting.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: AI ‘workslop’: The new productivity killer only training can stop
item.description: AI offers workers the promise of increased efficiency and productivity, freeing them up from repetitive work to tackle more complex tasks. But as companies have rolled out AI tools to employees, many are facing a different challenge: AI-generated work that does the opposite.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Qué medidas pueden tomar los CIO para progresar en la era de la IA
item.description: Si le pregunta a cualquier director de TI sobre la IA, probablemente le dirá que es ideal para realizar tareas repetitivas y de bajo nivel.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tesla recalls over 63,000 Cybertrucks due to the front lights being too bright
item.description: Tesla is recalling more than 63,000 Cybertrucks in the U.S. because the front lights are too bright, which may cause a distraction to other drivers and increase the risk of a collision.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AI browsers can be abused by malicious AI sidebar extensions: Report
item.description: AI browsers may be smart, but they’re not smart enough to block a common threat: Malicious extensions. That’s the conclusion of researchers at SquareX, who on Thursday released a report showing how attackers can exploit AI sidebars through compromised browser extensions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rivian mobility spinoff readies $4,500 e-bike as first product
item.description: Also, the micromobility startup spun out of Rivian Automotive Inc., has unveiled its first consumer product: an electric bike that will cost up to $4,500.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scammers are targeting cloud systems to make off with hauls of gift cards
item.description: Gift cards are ideal for hackers since they can easily be redeemed or sold without sharing personally identifiable data.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: From friendly text to financial trap: the new scam trend
item.description: Friendly text conversations about BBQs and social events can lead to WEEX gold trading scams that target older adults with fake investment opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elden Ring Tarnished Edition, FromSoftware's Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive version, has been delayed
item.description: FromSoftware has announced that it has delayed the Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive Elden Ring Tarnished Edition to 2026.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: How to use Python dataclasses
item.description: Everything in Python is an object, or so the saying goes. If you want to create your own custom objects, with their own properties and methods, you use Python’s class object to do it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cloud 2025
item.description: Unlock the future of cloud innovation The post Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cloud 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Google kills its cookie killer
item.description: Privacy Sandbox, Google’s attempt to create an alternative to cookies, looks like it has reached the end of the line. The company has announced that it is discontinuing 11 Privacy Sandbox technologies — pretty much the entire gamut.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Florida AG issues subpoena to Roblox for child protection policies
item.description: Attorney General James Uthmeier issued a subpoena to a popular online gaming platform, Roblox, on Monday, demanding information about how the platform sets age-verification requirements, moderates chat rooms, and markets itself to children.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less
item.description: "When you do any type of workforce reductions, you have to realize that you're also going to have attrition. Employees are going to leave," Rick Hammell said. The post With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Riverbed tackles AI data bottleneck with new Oracle-based service
item.description: Riverbed recently unveiled Data Express Service, a SaaS offering the company developed in partnership with Oracle that is designed to address the challenge of securely moving massive datasets quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AWS DNS error hits DynamoDB, causing problems for multiple services and customers
item.description: Monday got off to a bad start for Amazon Web Services users served by the company’s US-EAST-1 region, when a DNS problem rendered the DynamoDB API unreliable, with consequences for many AWS services and customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World
